entries.title: Nash Equilibrium
entries.content: A game theory concept where no player can benefit by changing their strategy unilaterally.
entries.example: Firms choosing prices in Bertrand competition

entries.title: Natural Logarithm
entries.content: Logarithm with base e (approx 2.71828).
entries.example: ln(10) approx 2.302

entries.title: Natural Numbers
entries.content: The set of positive integers used for counting.
entries.example: Numbers 1, 4, 100 are natural numbers

entries.title: Negation Logical
entries.content: An operator that flips the truth value of a proposition.
entries.example: Negation of 'It is raining' is 'It is not raining'

entries.title: Negative Exponent
entries.content: An exponent indicating reciprocal division rather than multiplication.

entries.title: Neighborhood in Topology
entries.content: A set containing an open set that contains the point.
entries.formula: N contains open U containing x
entries.example: An open interval around a point on a line

entries.title: Network Flow
entries.content: Directing flow through a network graph from source to sink under capacities.
entries.formula: sum of flow in = sum of flow out
entries.example: Maximizing data throughput in internet routing

entries.title: Newton-Raphson Method
entries.content: An iterative numerical method for finding roots of real-valued functions.
entries.example: Finding root of x^2 - 2 = 0 to approximate sqrt(2)

entries.title: Newton's Divided Differences
entries.content: An algorithm for calculating the coefficients of the interpolating polynomial.
entries.example: Interpolating a curve passing through 4 given points

entries.title: Nine-Point Circle
entries.content: A circle that can be constructed for any given triangle, passing through nine significant concyclic points.
entries.example: Drawing circle passing through midpoints of triangle sides

entries.title: Nominal Data
entries.content: A type of categorical data where values represent discrete labels with no intrinsic ordering.
entries.example: Categories like hair color or country names

entries.title: Non-Euclidean Geometry
entries.content: Geometries where Euclid's parallel postulate does not hold (e.g. spherical, hyperbolic).
entries.example: Angles of triangle on a sphere summing to >180 degrees

entries.title: Non-Singular Matrix
entries.content: An invertible square matrix with a non-zero determinant.
entries.example: Solving A*x = b uniquely as x = A^-1 * b

entries.title: Normal Distribution
entries.content: A symmetric bell-shaped continuous probability distribution.
entries.example: Distribution of SAT test scores

entries.title: Normal Distribution Standard
entries.content: Normal distribution with mean 0 and standard deviation 1.
entries.example: Using z-score tables to find probabilities

entries.title: Normal Line to Curve
entries.content: A line perpendicular to the tangent line at a point on a curve.
entries.example: Finding normal line to y = x^2 at (1, 1)

entries.title: Normal Subgroup
entries.content: A subgroup invariant under conjugation by elements of the parent group.
entries.example: Subgroups of Abelian groups are always normal

entries.title: Normal Vector
entries.content: A vector perpendicular to a surface or curve at a given point.
entries.example: Normal vector of plane ax+by+cz=d is [a, b, c]

entries.title: Null Hypothesis
entries.content: The hypothesis that there is no significant difference or effect, tested for rejection.
entries.example: Testing if a new drug has zero effect compared to standard

entries.title: Null Space of Matrix
entries.content: The vector space of all vectors that map to zero under matrix multiplication.
entries.example: Finding the kernel of a system of equations

entries.title: Number Line
entries.content: A visual representation of real numbers as points on a straight line.
entries.example: Placing fractions and integers visually on a scale

entries.title: Number Theory Prime Numbers
entries.content: The study of properties of prime numbers and integers.
entries.example: Investigating Goldbach's conjecture

entries.title: Numerator of Fraction
entries.content: The top number in a fraction, representing parts chosen.
entries.example: In 3/7, the numerator is 3

entries.title: Numerical Integration
entries.content: Algorithms for calculating the numerical value of a definite integral.
entries.example: Approximating area under e^(-x^2) from 0 to 1

entries.title: Numerical Analysis Bisection
entries.content: A root-finding algorithm that repeatedly bisects an interval and selects a subinterval.
entries.example: Finding where a polynomial crosses y=0 by bracket narrowing

entries.title: Numerical Analysis Secant
entries.content: A root-finding algorithm that uses a succession of roots of secant lines to approximate roots.
entries.example: Iterative root approximation without needing derivatives

entries.title: Net Present Value
entries.content: The present value of cash inflows minus outflows over time.
entries.example: Evaluating the profitability of a business project investment

entries.title: Nilpotent Matrix
entries.content: A square matrix N such that N^k = 0 for some positive integer k.
entries.example: A strictly upper triangular matrix is nilpotent

entries.title: Nondegenerate Matrix
entries.content: A matrix whose determinant is non-zero, or a bilinear form with no non-zero orthogonal vectors.
entries.example: Standard dot product is nondegenerate

entries.title: Nontrivial Solution
entries.content: A solution to a system of equations where at least one variable is non-zero.
entries.example: Solving A*x = 0 where x is not the zero vector

entries.title: Norm of Vector
entries.content: A function assigning a positive length or size to all vectors in a vector space.
entries.example: Euclidean norm or Manhattan norm of a vector

entries.title: Normalized Vector
entries.content: A vector scaled to have a length of exactly 1, also called a unit vector.
entries.example: Normalizing [3, 4] to [0.6, 0.8]

entries.title: Normal Distribution Z-score
entries.content: The number of standard deviations a data point is from the mean.
entries.example: Converting a test score of 85 to a z-score of 1.5

entries.title: Null Set Definition
entries.content: A set with no elements, or a set of measure zero in integration.
entries.formula: Empty set or set of measure 0
entries.example: The set of rational numbers has Lebesgue measure zero

entries.title: Number of Subsets Formula
entries.content: The number of subsets in a power set of a set with n elements is 2^n.
entries.example: A set of 3 elements has 8 subsets

entries.title: Numerical ODE Solving
entries.content: Using step algorithms to solve differential equations.
entries.example: Simulating planetary motion over time intervals

entries.title: Numerical Differentiation
entries.content: Estimating the derivative of a function using values at discrete points.
entries.example: Estimating velocity from GPS location logs

entries.title: Napierian Logarithm
entries.content: The original form of logarithms defined by John Napier, closely related to natural logarithms.
entries.example: Historical base calculations

entries.title: Natural Spline
entries.content: A piecewise polynomial interpolation curve that is twice continuously differentiable and has zero second derivatives at endpoints.
entries.example: Creating smooth curves through data points for graphics

entries.title: Net of 3D Shape
entries.content: A two-dimensional pattern that can be folded to form a three-dimensional solid.
entries.example: A cross pattern of six squares folding into a cube
